---
title: "Plan and Learning Objectives"
format: html
---

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
library(tidyverse)
```

# Practical 1 - Using R and RStudio

::: {.callout-tip .partmenu #parts-0100}

## Practical 1 - Using R and RStudio

### @sec-p1_r_and_rstudio

#### Sections
- @sec-r_language
- @sec-coding_in_the_console
- @sec-rstudio_interface
- @sec-console_scripts_quarto
- @sec-documenting_and_formatting_your_code
- @sec-summary_0101

#### Learning Objectives

By the end of this part of the practical you should be able to:

- Explain what R is and why it is useful for biological data analysis.
- Use some features of the RStudio interface
- Use the R console, scripts and Quarto documents to run R code.
- Create a simple reproducible analysis using Quarto.
- Understand the benefits of writing clear, well-formatted and documented R code.

### @sec-p2_functions_and_variables

#### Sections
- @sec-functions
- @sec-arguments
- @sec-variables
- @sec-variable_types
- @sec-summary_0102

#### Learning Objectives

By the end of this part of the practical you should be able to:

- Understand what a function is and use some simple functions.
- Understand how and why we provide arguments to functions.
- Explain the concept of a variable and use variables in your code.
- Know the difference between a character, numeric and logical variable.

### @sec-p3_vectors_data_frames_plots

#### Sections
- @sec-packages
- @sec-vectors
- @sec-data_frames
- @sec-csv
- @sec-plots
- @sec-summary_0103

By the end of this part of the practical you should be able to:

#### Learning Objectives

- Install and load a package in R
- Recognise and use the vector variable type.
- Isolate elements of the vector using an index.
- Create and view a simple data frame
- Recognise and use the CSV file type
- Plot a simple scatter plot in R

:::
